introSection: ThunderVolt is a steel launched roller coaster currently operating at Playland in Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride originally opened in 2009 with Miragica as Senzafiato, which is Italian for "breathless". The coaster closed in 2018 with the park and remained standing but not operating until it was removed in 2021. The ride was shipped to Zamperla, where it was renovated. Zamperla replaced the ride's hydraulic launch with an LSM launch, as well as built new trains. It was subsequently sold to Playland for CAD$9 million (USD$6.64 million) after these modifications were made, making it Playland's largest ride investment. The ride was installed where Corkscrew, Drop Zone, and The Revelation were formerly located. The ride is marketed as the fastest launch coaster in Canada.

One of the new cars for the ride was displayed at Zamperla's booth during the 2022 IAAPA Expo in Orlando, Florida, USA.

In March 2023, Playland hosted a contest on Namecontests.com to name the ride. Winners received two season passes and a chance to win two invitations to the ride's launch party. 852 entries were submitted.

The coaster reopened on 6 July 2024.

sections.title: Colour scheme
sections.content: The ride has yellow track, pink supports, and black cars. The ride used to feature red track and blue supports in Italy.

fullText:
ThunderVolt is a steel launched roller coaster currently operating at Playland in Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada.

The ride originally opened in 2009 with Miragica as Senzafiato, which is Italian for "breathless". The coaster closed in 2018 with the park and remained standing but not operating until it was removed in 2021. The ride was shipped to Zamperla, where it was renovated. Zamperla replaced the ride's hydraulic launch with an LSM launch, as well as built new trains. It was subsequently sold to Playland for CAD$9 million (USD$6.64 million) after these modifications were made, making it Playland's largest ride investment. The ride was installed where Corkscrew, Drop Zone, and The Revelation were formerly located. The ride is marketed as the fastest launch coaster in Canada.

One of the new cars for the ride was displayed at Zamperla's booth during the 2022 IAAPA Expo in Orlando, Florida, USA.

In March 2023, Playland hosted a contest on Namecontests.com to name the ride. Winners received two season passes and a chance to win two invitations to the ride's launch party. 852 entries were submitted.

The coaster reopened on 6 July 2024.



The ride has yellow track, pink supports, and black cars. The ride used to feature red track and blue supports in Italy.




 * Zamperla / Playland Launch Coaster Concept
